title: camissoniopsis cheiranthifolia subsp. cheiranthifolia
intro: Camissoniopsis cheiranthifolia subsp. cheiranthifolia, also known as the California poppy or California goldenpoppy, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and Baja California. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and soothing digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Camissoniopsis cheiranthifolia subsp. cheiranthifolia include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to leverage its medicinal properties.
